title: acmena lanceolata
intro: Acmena lanceolata, also known as the lilly pilly or cherry ballart, is a species of flowering plant native to Australia and New Caledonia. It has been traditionally used in Australian medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, and has been shown to have potential health benefits, including the treatment of colds and flu, as well as soothing irritated skin. The medicinal properties of the plant are primarily attributed to its high content of vitamin C and anthocyanins, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. In herbal preparations, the leaves and berries of the lilly pilly are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
